<!--- One sentence to describe the purpose of this PR, refer to any linked issues:
#14 -- this will link to issue 14

We don't know why, but aquaplanet_nonequil_allsky_gw_res and aquaplanet_equil_allsky_gw_raw_zonalasym seemed to not produce the same result between the merge-queue branch and the main branch. See https://buildkite.com/clima/climaatmos-ci/builds/23508. Try to fix it by bumping the ref counter.

title: bump ref counter

body: ClimaCoupler handles restarts in its own way, but it was not possible to start a simulation from a `t_start` that is not zero without using the ClimaAtmos restart system. This commit adds this option and moves `t_start` inside `sim_info` for symmetry with `t_end` and `dt`.
title: Add option to restart from a non zero t and bump to 0.29.1

body:
This PR:
- adds the contribution from sedimenting cloud condensate to `surface_rain_flux` and `surface_snow_flux` for the 1M microphysics. I would prefer to keep the names the same even though now they contain both precipitation and cloud fluxes. It's a slow process to change it in both Coupler and Atmos.
title: Surface cloud sedimentation included in precipitation fluxes

body:
<!--- THESE LINES ARE COMMENTED -->
## Purpose 
<!--- One sentence to describe the purpose of this PR, refer to any linked issues:
#14 -- this will link to issue 14
Closes #2 -- this will automatically close issue 2 on PR merge
-->
Fixes the [broken downgrade test](https://github.com/CliMA/ClimaAtmos.jl/actions/runs/13974091895/job/39123240375). 
## Content
<!---  specific tasks that are currently complete 
- Solution implemented
-->
This was caused by a trailing comma in a YAML file `[pr, ]` which resulted in an error on an old version of YAML.jl (v0.4.0).
Maybe we should add a YAML formatter/linter.
